name: project-runtime-state
question: Does this project have live dev servers, shared build artifacts, file watchers, or stateful runtimes that would conflict under PODS worktree isolation?

schema.hasLiveDevServer: boolean (Dev Port Registry consulted; at least one assigned port currently bound)
schema.devPortRegistryEntries: array of {project, port, source} (project assignments parsed from ~/.claude/MACHINE_CONFIG.md Dev Port Registry section, scoped to current workDir when resolvable)
schema.sharedBuildArtifacts: array of strings (detected paths: node_modules, target, .cargo, .gradle, vendor, .venv, etc.)
schema.fileWatchers: array of strings (detected configs: vite.config.{js,ts,mjs}, nodemon.json, webpack.config.*, tsup.config.*, etc.)
schema.statefulRuntime: string|null (one of: tauri, electron, expo, django-runserver, next.js-dev, vite-dev, ...; null if none detected)
schema.antiFitScore: integer 0-5 (sum of indicator counts; >=3 = strong anti-fit signal for worktree mode)
schema.antiFitReasons: array of strings (one human-readable string per active anti-fit indicator)

consultation._note: Heuristic-only consumer (WT-06 in `/0-uldf-pods-parallelize` analysis). Not surfaced at session-start; not auto-cached. Each call re-probes Dev Port Registry liveness and re-globs build/watcher artifacts.

fallback: Read ~/.claude/MACHINE_CONFIG.md Dev Port Registry section manually; check whether any assigned port is bound (lsof -i / Get-NetTCPConnection); inspect package.json/Cargo.toml/pyproject.toml for tauri/electron/expo/django; eyeball node_modules/, target/, .gradle/, vendor/, .venv/.

provenance.note: Project-state oracle that pre-answers the worktree-mode fit question. Consumer is the proactive heuristic in `claude-template/segments/-pods/parallelize_analysis.md` Step 6 (WT-06). Output schema frozen at v1; future indicators land as additive fields with backward-compat fixtures. PODS opt-in worktree mode Arc 1 (DEC-61, 2026-05-10).
